column2: This great nation had been occupied by the Axis. After some years of struggle liberating themselves, and with the Allies' assistance, the fighters for freedom have at last liberated their capital.
The Korean independence movement is boiling. We should make a decision. Releasing Korea as a puppet would allow us to incorporate it into our Greater East Asia Co-prosperity Sphere.

In February 1945, Soviet, American and British officials met in Yalta and agreed on the basics on Poland's future borders. In the east, the British agreed to the Curzon Line. In the west, Poland should receive part of East Prussia, Danzig, the eastern tip of Pomerania and Upper Silesia.
Yalta conference - the Soviet Union's rush to Germany
Yalta conference - the Western Allies' rush to Germany

During the on-going Pacific War, the leaders of the United States, the United Kingdom, and the Republic of China met in Cairo, Egypt in November, 1943. They agreed with the following points: (1) Japan be stripped of all the islands in the Pacific which she had seized or occupied since the beginning of the First World War in 1914. (2) All the territories Japan had taken from the Chinese would be also restored to the Republic of China. (3) Korea would become free and independent.

The Potsdam Declaration or the Proclamation Defining Terms for Japanese Surrender is a statement that called for the Surrender of the Empire of Japan during World War II. On July 26th, 1945, United States President Harry S. Truman, United Kingdom Prime Minister Winston Churchill (ratified by new Prime Minister Clement Attlee), and Chairman of the Nationalist Government of China Chiang Kai-shek issued the document, which outlined the terms of surrender for the Empire of Japan as agreed upon at the Potsdam Conference. This ultimatum stated that, if Japan did not surrender, it would face "prompt and utter destruction", although the document did not make any mention of atomic weapons.\n\nThe occupying forces of the Allies shall be withdrawn from Japan as soon as these objectives have been accomplished and there has been established, in accordance with the freely expressed will of the Japanese people, a peacefully inclined and responsible government.

After Germany's unconditional surrender on 8 May 1945, the Allies divided "Occupation Zone Germany" into four military occupation zones, the French in the southwest, the British in the northwest, the United States in the south, and the Soviets in the east.

The occupying forces of the Allies shall be withdrawn from Japan as soon as these objectives have been accomplished and there has been established, in accordance with the freely expressed will of the Japanese people, a peacefully inclined and responsible government.

Aboard the USS Missouri in Tokyo Bay, Japan formally surrenders to the Allies, bringing an end to the long and bloody struggle in the Pacific theater.

By the summer of 1945, the defeat of Japan was a foregone conclusion. The Japanese navy and air force were destroyed. The Allied naval blockade of Japan and intensive bombing of Japanese cities had left the country and its economy devastated. At the end of June, the Americans captured Okinawa, a Japanese island from which the Allies could launch an invasion of the main Japanese home islands. U.S. General Douglas MacArthur was put in charge of the invasion, which was code-named "Operation Olympic" and set for November 1945.

However, after the dropping of two atomic bombs and the Soviet invasion of Manchuria, the Japanese government surrendered, rendering the planned invasion of the Japanese home islands unnecessary.
It has been a long struggle, indeed.
The Sun Sets in the Pacific

The Empire of Japan is no more. Unconditional surrender is at hand.

They shall grow not old, as we that are left grow old:
Age shall not weary them, nor the years condemn.
At the going down of the sun and in the morning,
We will remember them.

-- Laurence Binyon
